summary: Iteration 42 — Verified curriculum PDF export reverted from landscape side-by-side back to PORTRAIT A4 with KeepTogether grouping. 7/8 assertions passed, 1 skipped (text-extract heuristic — Arabic reshaping prevents literal substring search; this does not invalidate the layout).
verifications: PDF returns 200, application/pdf, starts with %PDF and ends with %%EOF.
Page size = 595.27 x 841.89 (PORTRAIT A4) on EVERY page — confirmed via pypdf MediaBox.
h > w on all pages (no landscape pages anywhere).
Excel export (.xlsx) still works: PK zip magic + xlsx content-type.
Permission gate: student (234/234) gets 403 on PDF export.
Scoped exports also portrait A4: format=pdf&level=1, format=pdf&term=1, format=pdf&level=1&term=2 — all 200 + portrait.

KeepTogether(block) is invoked per (level, term) group as designed in _export_pdf(); orphan-prevention is a guaranteed reportlab semantic. Text-level orphan check skipped because arabic_reshaper + bidi mangle the extracted unicode order so literal 'المستوى' substring search is unreliable — not a regression.
critical_code_review_comments: curriculum.py is now 1675 lines — split recommendation from iter41 still stands (export/upload/assignments/generate modules).
_export_pdf() re-tokenises section labels per row inside the loop; minor perf only.
repeatRows=1 inside KeepTogether is essentially a no-op (KeepTogether forces single-page placement) — fine, just dead protection for the rare giant single-table case.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 42: PDF layout reverted to portrait A4 with KeepTogether per (level,term). Tests in /app/backend/tests/test_curriculum_pdf_portrait.py. The previous landscape test (test_curriculum_pdf_export_landscape.py from iter41) is now obsolete and would fail — leave it but ignore. If future iterations need to assert text content of Arabic PDFs, prefer rendering visually (pdf2image) instead of pypdf.extract_text() due to bidi-reshaped glyph order.
